entry.title: supplier invoice foreign currency
entry.content:
Where can the exchange rate be updated so that the valuation of an invoice recorded in foreign currency is registered in our company currency according to the supplier's invoice valuation?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: supplier invoice foreign currency
entry.content:
Where to update the exchange rate:

1. Supplier Invoice Document Itself
When manually creating or editing a supplier invoice, you can manually override the exchange rate in the “Valuation” tab or “Prices and Taxes” tab (depending on the UI).

This sets the specific exchange rate for that invoice, ensuring your company currency valuation reflects the supplier's invoice valuation.

2. Exchange Rate Table (General Ledger Work Center)
Path: General Ledger → Master Data → Exchange Rates

You can update or maintain daily or periodic exchange rates for currency pairs .

These rates are automatically used if no manual override is done during invoice posting.

3. Business Configuration (for Source of Exchange Rate)
You can configure which source of exchange rate should be prioritized .

Path: Business Configuration → Overview → Search “Exchange Rate Type”

4. Invoice via Purchase Order
If the invoice is created via a PO, and no exchange rate is entered in the invoice, the system uses the exchange rate valid on the invoice document date from the exchange rate table.

Kindly have a look into the below help doc: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2c2cb764722d101483aca5ac7b49067b.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Handover of test cases to test from one user to another user SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Dear,

The creation of a test case and link to a test plan and execute is clear, only to have in really practice is different, hopefully you will able to give us more clearity as we are going to use SAP Cloud ALM for each project. We are going to start with the test execution and have created multiple test cases, we have now questions regrading test execution and test plans. Any suggestion are welcome:

  1. When creating a test case we have the possibility to create a test plan and link the test case to these test plan, but when linking multiple test case to a test plan we cannot define the order/sequence of the test cases.
  2. We have only the possibility to add one tester in the test plan? What if I have more testers to test on one teste case ?
  3. If we assign a tester does he receive any e-mail? 
  4. Is there any e-mail chain/notification regarding test management except defects?
  5. How can we handover a certain test case/execution to another (does he also receive an e-mail) ?  

Thank you in advance and kind regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Handover of test cases to test from one user to anoth...
entry.content:
Manual Test Cases are not designed to have a sequence of execution between each other. There is no enforced execution sequence dependency. Manual Test Cases can be executed in any order. 

If you need to enforce a sequence dependency, then you can write the first step of a Test Case so that it outlines that another Test Case must first be completed as a prerequisite. Again, SAP Cloud ALM Test Execution cannot enforce this, your users will need to abide by the steps.

The display order/sequence of the manual Test Cases is only visual in the Test Preparation and Test Execution apps. It is not intended as an order of execution. The display order can be changed by clicking the "Sort" icon in the result table (two vertical arrows, one pointing down, the other pointing up). Multiple field values are available as sort options.

Test Cases aren't assigned to individual users, Test Plans are. In Test Planning, the "responsible" person is the tester. The search field "Tester" in the Test Execution app will be the same value as the assigned "responsible" person for the Test Plan. It is not currently possible to assign multiple users as being responsible for a Test Plan, only a single user can be assigned. If you need to change the tester for a Test Plan (and all its Test Cases), you must change the "responsible" person for the Test Plan.

KBA 3390449 outlines the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation use cases that support notifications (including e-mails). Currently Test Management functionality is not included in this list.

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and provide direction to our Developers in relation to any of these topics, please log a request in the SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation Customer Influence campaign.

entry.title: Re: Order siblings in a Hierarchy with Directory with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog Stefan! Very useful indeed. 

We tried to remote tables as you mentioned towards the end with deriving the sequence from the NEXTID. We didn't find a solution for getting it done via a sqlscript view though. We couldn't find a solution that doesn't require recursion to "disassemble" the daisy chain and I think DSP sqlscript doesn't support recursion. Are we missing something there?

We ended up going forward with the CDS option that you also mentioned to get remote tables working.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Message Popover in Standard Pick by Cart F...
entry.content:
Please see the binding and context associated with Vaibhav,

console.log(sap.ui.getCore().getMessageManager().getMessageModel().getData()); 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to Use Dynamic Pictures on Document Generation
entry.content:
Hello,

I kindly ask you this question:

I did follow those fantastic steps so I had my (several) templates with (several) dynamic images.

Unfortunately, I have to move those templates in multiple instances but the logos are of course missing the the other instances because the URL does not work (E.g.: I created the templates in a DEV instance and I moved them in TEST where the images are missing).

What can I do?

Is there a "repository" in SF where I can bring back those images?

Thanks

entry.title: Need help understanding meter reading reason 11 and 16
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I understand that some meter reading reasons are generated automatically during various processes, such as installations or removals. Both MR reason 11 and 16 seem to be related to programming—either before or after it.

I’ve noticed that meter readings with reason 11 appear before those with reason 16.
Could someone explain the relationship between these two reasons and why this ordering occurs?
An explanation of what each of these reasons is used for would also be appreciated.

Best regards,
ud39

entry.title: How to check if expected data are released from Universal Journals to Group reporting(FIN-CS-COR-DT)
entry.content:
1) check if GRPL is activated (t-cd: CXB3)
1.1) if GRPL is activated, then please check in t-cd: FINCS_MAP_RLDNR_R,  get the Ledger which used for GRPL.
1.2) If GRPL is not activated, please check field value TF200-RLDNR_SOURCE to get the Ledger which used for data source.

2) Check in table FINCS_BUNIT , T001.  From table FINCS_BUNIT, you can get the assigned company for the cons.unit. And then use it to check in table T001 with RCOMP = company, with this you will know the company code(BUKRS)
3) With  ledger, company code, you may go to t-cd: FAGLB03, to check the balance for specific account in each month. Example below:


4) On the other hands, you may go to the data monitor, and run data release in test mode for specific cons.unit and fiscal year/period.  From the results screen, you can check the the account balance for by click on the toggle button. Please check the picture below:


5) Another approach to check the account balance is the app "Group Data analysis".
Input value for filter "Cons.unit" and "Document type = 0F", and in the result list, add "Account number".


6) If you found that the balance in FAGLB03 does not corresponding to the results in Group data analysis. (in my example, the balance is -2000 in FAGLB03 for 12530000 , but in GDA, the balance is -1000 in FAGLB03 for 12530000), then you will need to do data analysis in order to find the reason for the difference.
In that case, please refer to my blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-check-the-reason-when-some-items-are-not-released-from-acdoca-to/ba-p/14088012

entry.title: Switch from SAP_COM_0086 (Neo) to SAP_COM_0623 (Cloud Foundry)
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will guide you through the process of switching from the theming communication scenario SAP_COM_0086 (SAP BTP, Neo environment) to SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment). This switch enables you to access the latest features of the UI theme designer, such as theme migration.

Background

In an S/4HANA Cloud system, you use the UI theme designer from either the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment or the SAP BTP, Neo environment to create and manage custom themes for SAP applications. To do this, you must implement one of the communication scenarios for the UI theme designer. For more details, refer to the documentation on Integrating UI Theme Designer.

Why is it important to switch to the communication scenario SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment)?

  • Sunsetting of SAP BTP, Neo Environment: The SAP BTP, Neo environment will be retired by the end of 2028. For more information, refer to the blog post Farewell, Neo!
  • End-of-Life for Belize Theme: The Belize theme has reached its end-of-life with SAPUI5 1.135. By using the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, you can migrate your custom themes to modern themes based on Quartz or Horizon. For further details, see:
    • SAP note 3595596.
    • Blog posts: Removal of Belize Theme of SAP Fiori and Upcoming Removal of SAP Fiori Themes Belize and Blue Crystal.

Prerequisites

  • You already have a subaccount on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment.

Procedure

Step 1: Initial Setup

  1. Set up the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment as described in the guide Initial Setup of UI Theme Designer: Portal Scenario.

Step 2: Copy Themes

  1. Export your custom themes from the UI theme designer in the SAP BTP, Neo environment. For detailed instructions, refer to Exporting Themes.
  2. Import your custom themes into the UI theme designer in the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment. For detailed instructions, refer to Importing Themes.
  3. If your custom theme is based on the Belize theme, migrate it to a theme based on Horizon or Quartz. See Migrating Themes for further information.
    Note: The Belize theme has been deprecated since SAPUI5 version 1.120 and was removed in SAPUI5 version 1.135.
  4. After a successful migration, rename your theme accordingly. Use the same theme ID as before to ensure that no changes are needed for users to continue using the theme. For detailed instructions, refer to Renaming Themes.
  5. Test your theme using the preview pages available in the UI theme designer. For detailed instructions, refer to Editing Themes.
    Note: Migration will keep as many of your changes as possible, such as changed parameters or uploaded resources like a logo. However, there will be cases where the result is not 100% correct. Revisit the migration result and use it as a starting point for manual fine-tuning.
  6. Publish your theme to make it available for use. For detailed instructions, refer to Publishing Themes.

Step 3: Switch Communication Scenario

Important: Perform this step during a maintenance window when few or no users are active in the system. While switching the communication scenario, applications will temporarily display the SAP standard theme.

  1. Delete the communication scenario SAP_COM_0086 for the SAP BTP, Neo environment.
  2. Create a new communication scenario for SAP_COM_0623 (SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment) as described in the guide SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment (SAP_COM_0623).
  3. Test your custom themes with the SAP Fiori Launchpad in the S/4HANA Cloud system.

Recap

By following the outlined steps—setting up the UI theme designer on the SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment, migrating your themes, and switching the communication scenario—you can modernize your theming approach and ensure a seamless transition with minimal disruption to your users.

For additional information, refer to the linked SAP Notes and guides. If you need further assistance, feel free to reach out via component CA-UI2-THD.

entry.title: The attachments in the business partner master data in sap will be lost after being uploaded
entry.content: After I uploaded an attachment in the business partner master data, the record of the attachment would disappear after some time. However, when I queried the database, I found that this atcentry had a record, but I couldn't find this record in the atc1 table. I can find these files in the attachment folder of the server. Why is that? The version of sap I'm using is sap b1 10.00.250

entry.title: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content:
Hi guys,
I have one demo process in my SAP Build Process Automation Application and I have deployed and published it.


Now, in my SAP Build Apps, I have created an application and in Integration tab, I selected SAP Build Library, I can see my process there, but when I click on it shows Page not found.


I have also configured sap_process_automation_service_user_access destination in my BTP for the instance of Build Process automation and test shows successful & 401. Also, added that destination in control tower's destination in build lobby. Please help.

In the attached image we can see the published process available in cloud library.

SAP Build Library

Page Not Found Error for process

Thanks & Regards,
Marsh

entry.title: Join Our Espresso Session on May 13th: 🌿 Experience the SAP Energy Park
entry.content:
Dear Energy Community,

We are delighted to offer another 30-minute Espresso Session for you on 

📅Date: Tuesday, 13 May 2025
🕓Time: 16:00 CET

👉 Register here

In the face of growing environmental challenges posed by traditional energy sources and the urgent need for sustainable energy practices, SAP is setting a powerful example with its innovative SAP Energy Park initiative.

By leveraging SAP solutions to support the energy transition on their campus, SAP is driving significant change. 🌍

Join us to explore how the SAP Energy Park initiative aims to not only transform our own operations but also empower other organizations to embrace the energy transition through a combination of cutting-edge technology, innovative real-life scenarios, and strategic partnerships. 🚀

In this session, you will discover:

🌱The Motivation Behind SAP's Initiative:
Understanding the critical need for decarbonization and the emergence of prosumers in the energy sector.

🔍An Overview of the SAP Energy Park:
Exploring the value proposition and various use cases of the initiative.

💡Experiencing the SAP Energy Park:
Gain valuable insights and information.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

🎤Speakers:

Bruno Pincolini
Business Development Energy & Utilities, SAP

Kim Maren Ekrutt
Head Of Innovation & Sustainability for Energy & Utilities, SAP

Don't miss this opportunity to learn how SAP is leading the way in sustainable energy practices and discover how your organization can benefit from these innovations. Mark your calendar and join us for an insightful and inspiring webinar!

In this blog post, you can read about the SAP Energy Park. 

👉 Register now and be part of the energy revolution! 👈

entry.title: Re: Users Cannot See Their Own Contingent Worker Profile...
entry.content: Could you solve this isue? I have the same scenario

entry.title: Re: API to POST to SAP | RAP | SEGW | if_http_extension~h...
entry.content:
root:-

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Event Root'
@ObjectModel.compositionRoot: true
define view entity ZI_Event
  as select from zevent
{
  key id    as EventId,
      event,
      @ObjectModel.association.type: [#TO_COMPOSITION_CHILD]
      _Payer
}

Thanks

entry.title: MRP live considering takt time
entry.content:
Specifically, this concerns the takt time including the lot-sizing procedure (it's in the MRP1 view of the material master data). Currently, the MRP Live doesn't support the takt time, and you're forced to use MRP Classic for the corresponding materials.

Is there any plan from SAP about whether and when the takt time might also be available with MRP Live, or will be disabled as functionality because of it’s based on old techniques and not in scope or part of SAP’ s roadmap?

Many thanks’ in advance for your support, I’m looking forward to  any feedback.

entry.title: Upload/Create Logistics Area in Bulk in Business ByDesign.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We are looking for standard upload function in ByD where we can upload/create bin locations in bulk against the location as the number of count for bin location against each location is high  and we have multiple locations,

Please let us know if we can upload/create bin location via excel template in ByD.

Regards,
Vimal ,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Upload/Create Logistics Area in Bulk in Business ByDe...
entry.content:
Dear Vimal, 

There is no option to perform mass upload of logistics areas via Excel. However, you can create multiple logistics areas via the below given steps in the system.

  1. Go to Supply Chain Design Master Data
  2. Navigate to the Locations view.
  3. Search and select the relevant Site.
  4. Choose the Edit Site Layout option.
  5. Select the Add Multiple Logistics Areas button.
  6. Enter the Pattern ID, From and To Logistics Area IDs and Reference Logistics area from which the new logistics areas/bins should be created.
  7. Choose the OK button.
  8. Review the list of Logistics Areas created and choose the Save button.

Note: Choose the Logistics Area for reference from under the same site.

Regards, 
Anand
SAP Technical Support

entry.title: Fiori Elements (RAP) vs Freestyle UI5 – Best fit for complex UI with IconTabs and F4 help?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are evaluating whether to go for Fiori Elements using RAP (OData V4) or a Freestyle SAPUI5 application for a new requirement.

Here’s the context:

  • OData V4 service is already available, built using the ABAP RAP model.

  • The required UI layout includes:

    • A custom header at the top

    • A SplitApp layout below

    • On the right pane, multiple IconTab filters

    • Input fields with F4 Help inside each tab

Given the flexibility needed in the layout and UI interactions (especially for SplitApp and F4 integration inside tabs), I’m trying to decide whether this is better suited for:

🔹 Fiori Elements + RAP + Annotations + Custom sections (Flexible Programming Model)
or
🔹 Freestyle SAPUI5 with smart controls or custom controls

Questions:

  1. Would Fiori Elements (Object Page or Flexible Column Layout) allow such a layout with minimal custom sections?

  2. How well does the annotation-driven model support IconTab structures and embedded F4 help in nested sections?

  3. Are there limitations or known workarounds for achieving SplitApp-like behavior in Fiori Elements?

Looking forward to expert advice and experience-based suggestions.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Business context of Production Order while creating the custom field in public cloud
entry.content:
Can you suggest a business context to create a custom field in the production order CO02 in public cloud.

 

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Business context of Production Order while creating t...
entry.content:
The correct business context is FINS_ORDER which uses below interfaces:

  • Manage Production Orders (Fiori)
  • Manage Process Orders (Fiori)
  • Production Order Header (SAP GUI)
  • Process Order Header (SAP GUI)

 refer to the link provided : App Extensibility in Production Operations

entry.title: How to fetch the data from a page in SAC dashboard for further use?
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

As I’m new to SAP Analytics Cloud, could you advise on fetching data from dashboards (single charts or entire pages)? I want to utilize this data for further use.

  1. Is scripting required, if Yes, how I can do that?

  2. Supported formats (Excel, CSV, etc.)?

  3. Any automation/governance best practices?

A brief demo or tips would be helpful. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Order siblings in a Hierarchy with Directory with SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @TApen sqlscript supports a WHILE loop, which allows you to traverse siblings as long as there is a NEXTID left. That should work right?

entry.title: Connectivity Tests Before Executing Hybrid Testing with CAP: Connecting to SAP S/4HANA On-Premise
entry.content:
Introduction

In this article, I will explain "Hybrid Testing" in the context of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), which allows development while accessing real data from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise. I will focus on connectivity tests that are useful before executing Hybrid Testing. For detailed information on setting up the environment, please refer to the links below.

  • SAP Gateway - Activate and Maintain Services
  • Discovery Center - Connect S/4HANA system using Cloud Connector
  • SAP CAP  - Consuming Services #Connect to Remote Services Locally
  • SAP CAP - Hybrid Testing
  • SAP Cloud SKD - Connecting to External Systems From the Business Application Studio #Connection to a...

What is Hybrid Testing?

In app development using CAP, testing is typically conducted in a local environment using mock data. However, there are cases where you need to access cloud or on-premise services. "Hybrid Testing" is a useful method in such cases. By using this testing method, developers can run applications in a local environment while connecting to remote services, thereby improving development efficiency.

Challenges of Hybrid Testing with SAP S/4HANA On-Premise

While there are advantages, accessing on-premise systems relies on services such as SAP Destination Service, SAP Connectivity Service, and Cloud Connector. This makes the environment setup more complex compared to local testing with mock data. Therefore, I introduce connectivity tests that can help streamline the environment setup process.

Connectivity Tests

The connectivity tests introduced are in the order shown in the diagram below. I recommend testing them sequentially, starting with those with fewer dependencies.

1. Connectivity Test from SAP Gateway Client

The SAP Gateway Client, with T-code /IWFND/GW_CLIENT, functions as an HTTP(S) client to test OData services. After entering the appropriate HTTP method, OData query (format, top...), request URI, request headers like Content-Type, and payload, click "Execute" on the app to check the availability and response of the target OData service within S/4HANA.

If an error occurs, verify that the OData service is activated and that the correct path is specified, among other potential issues.

2. Connectivity Test from Cloud Connector Administration UI

After a successful test from the Gateway Client, test the connectivity from SCC to the on-premise system. Specifically, click the leftmost icon in Actions to verify the connection from the Virtual host defined in SCC to the Internal host (on-premise). If 'Reachable' is displayed, the test is successful.

3. Connectivity Test from BTP Cockpit

In the BTP Cockpit, perform a "Check Connection" to SCC to verify if the URL of the Destination can be reached. Note that this test does not guarantee that the Virtual host of the Destination Service (SCC) is operational, but only confirms reachability.

'The check does not guarantee that the target system or service is operational. It only verifies if ...

If an error occurs, there may be issues with the SCC settings, version, or server availability.

4. Connectivity Test from SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

Finally, perform Hybrid Testing itself. Bind the destination and auth services to the app deployed in the Cloud Foundry Runtime, and execute the following command to make an HTTP request from the HTTP file.

# JavaScript
cds watch --profile hybrid

# TypeScript
cds-ts watch --profile hybrid

Notes:

  1. When accessing SCC from a local machine using Hybrid Testing, it is necessary to make calls from within the BTP environment. Therefore, please use BAS instead of IDEs like VS Code. Reference link
  2. In the .env file, you need to add the destinations variable by obtaining the name and URL information of the destination from the Destinations page in the BTP Cockpit.
destinations=[{"name":"demodestination","proxyHost":"http://127.0.0.1","proxyPort":"8887","url":"http://demodestination.dest"}]

Conclusion

I hope this helps you in your app development using CAP.

entry.title: Re: How to fetch the data from a page in SAC dashboard fo...

Hi

Please try 

Export via Excel

entry.title: DCD for Deleting Delivery or PGI
entry.content:
Hi All,

Our credit check triggers at the time of PGI. Recently our SD team has set a process that will allow user to delete a PGI/Delivery document. However I am seeing when a PGI is deleted, the downstream DCD created is not deleted. As a result the exposure at the BP level remains unchanged which affects the credit check for other orders. Also on the tech side, theres workflow generating continiously which keeps failing. I have to manually then remove those items under 400 commitment category manually.

Is there any config that will not allow user to delete a PGI or once PGI is deleted DCD is also deleted ? This behaviour between FSCM and SD doesnt looks very consistent. Is there any available config to control this or is it a validation rule that we need to set ?

Regds

Subha

entry.content:
Hi,

Looks like target-mapping issues(hence been redirected to home).

In SAP Build Work Zone, confirm that the target mapping for your semantic object and action is correctly set up and assigned to the necessary roles or groups

please match(case-sensitive) intent with what is mentioned in the manifest.json too.

thanks

entry.title: Re: API to POST to SAP | RAP | SEGW | if_http_extension~h...
entry.content: Thanks Arun. No, I have to write logic/use BAPIs to update some standard tables. The JSON payload doesn't link to any table in the DB. It will be a lot tidier with SEGW/HTTP Handler. 😞

entry.title: How to check failed log, status of "integration status" only in Ariba ?
entry.content:
Now, it happened a issue on Ariba SM administrator.
For detail,
by using "Ariba > SM administrator > Integration message status"
we found some failed status.

So, we have to do "reprocess" now.
After that we need to check status whether there is any data of failed status.

So please help to good methods in this issue.
Our demands are
1. We are in charge of maintenance.
2. We must not chang data of users.
3. If we have "SM ERP Administorator" in Ariba, we will be able to do "reprocess". We would like to be able to check failed status only.

Our thinking are that
#1 you should change "send daily notifications of synchoronazation failures" in "Ariba > SM administrator > Set up ERP integration".
#2 The user(system administorator) will be set "SM ERP Administration".
#3 The user's email will be our e-mail address and we cannot use the user in Ariba.
#4 The user is locked.

Our maitenance persons do not have authority "SM ERP Administration" but we can find this issue by email with these thinking?
or
are there best authority(group), we have best authority(only to check failed status)?

In the first place, will be this solution of "send daily notifications of synchronization failures" in "Ariba > SM administrator > Set up ERP integration" solved for our issue("Ariba > SM administrator > Integration message status", we found some failed status.) ?

For your refrence, SM-12249: Enhancements to ERP integration error handling and error display for supplier data | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Certified SAP HA options based on products
entry.content:
Introduction

High Availability (HA) ensures a system remains operational despite internal failures, allowing IT maintenance on Infrastructure, OS, and application level without business user disruption. This 24/7 accessibility is achieved by eliminating single points of failure, typically aiming for 99.99% uptime. In SAP, HA is often implemented through server clustering, storage replication with each method varying by product.

For example, Red Hat HA utilizes server clustering and resources like Virtual IPs, STONITH, ASCS/SCS, ERS and Database resources to manage master/slave roles. Windows clustering achieves HA by moving quorum disks between servers. While SUSE, RHEL, and Windows are well-known SAP HA options, other SAP-certified solutions exist, which is mentioned below, 

Environment
SAP NetWeaver and HANA. 

Available options by products

 

Sl.NoVendor Product Name 
1Red HatPacemaker cluster 
2SUSEPacemaker cluster
3MicrosoftWindows server Failover cluster
4FujitsuDynamic Infrastructure Solutions for SAP(FlexFrame)
5IBMSystem z
Power System on AIX
Power System on Linux
IBM i
6NECExpresscluster
7Oracle Oracle Solaris Cluster
SAPCTL with Oracle Clusterware and Oracle Real Application Clusters
8ProLionClusterLion for SAP
9Veritas Technologies LLC InfoScale Enterprise

Reference - #https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/si/3362959203.html

entry.title: Re: How to use HTTP Post method in SAP Gateway Client using JSON format?
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi thank you. However, I am experiencing an error 400 code when using the PATCH, or DELETE HTTP Method. Would that be related to authorization issue?

entry.title: Re: Disable checkbox selection list report page
entry.content:
Hi,

Expression binding could you please try?

<CheckBox 
            selected="{YourEntitySet/CheckboxSelected}" 
            enabled="{= ${YourEntitySet/Condition} === 'EnabledValue' ? true : false}" />

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Issue with Message Popover in Standard Pick by Cart F...
entry.content:

Model and binding looks good.

entry.title: Re: Credit checks available in SAP Credit Management (FSCM)
entry.content:
Hi @edina_j0zs4 ,

If I want to implement a custom credit check/rule in public cloud, any idea on how to achieve that (or if that is even possible)? 

I've checked and I don't find the Badis mentioned here in the API hub (so not maybe released for cloud development)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Extensibility

entry.title: Re: the usage of additional measures in app manage KPI and report
entry.content:
Hello,

Here's the information the experts have provided.

Additional Measures: You can choose an additional set of measures for the KPI from the value help for the Additional Measures field. All measures available in the entity set are available for selection in the value help for Additional Measures field. The formatting applied to the main measure (Value Measure) applies to the additional measures at runtime. For example, if you have defined Percent as scaling and 0.0 as decimal precision for the main measure, then the same holds true for the additional measures you've defined.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Disable checkbox selection list report page
entry.content: Hi @ArunJacob , As this is list report template expression binding is not possible .. It is possible only in case of freestyle UI5 app

entry.title: Re: Final Review Step in Onboarding 2.0 Process
entry.content:
@fransvanderme were you able to fetch custom Mdfs as well in final review step ?
If yes please let us know the approach

entry.title: Using the online text repository instead of text elements in ABAP
entry.content:
Why quit using text elements?

Text elements are really old-school. They are stored with a report or a class and are accessible by other parts of the application only indirectly. There are are approaches to avoid these problems (see this old blog of mine). However, the online text repository (OTR) frame offers a more modern way to handle texts. Here some points that made me switch to OTR:

  • Text elements have meaningless key (text-010). Only using a literal in the coding or leaving a comment will make the coding readable
  • Text elements are stored with a program (or class). As soon as you start refactoring and extracting classes, you have to move the text elements (or store all text elements in a separate class es described in the blog mentioned above)
  • Text elements are of limited length
  • Text elements have an unhandy fixed length type, which makes it necessary to convert the values when passing them to string parameters

With OTR texts, all the above is resolved. However, the coding blows up a bit. Instead of writing

logger->add( 'Something's wrong'(001) ).

You have to code something like this:

logger->add( new CL_BSP_GET_TEXT_BY_ALIAS(
 )->get_text(
      language = sy-langu
      alias = 'ZDV24_TEXT_ACCESS_DEMO/EXAMPLE' ) ).

I created a small framework for this in order to streamline the use. It is available for download on github. With this, the coding turns out to be a bit friendlier:

class text definition inheriting from zcl_otr_util_demo_text_enum.
endclass.

class app definition inheriting from zcl_otr_util_demo_texts.
  public section.
    methods main.
endclass.

CLASS app IMPLEMENTATION.
  method main.
    cl_demo_output=>display( get_text( text=>the_text ) ).
  endmethod.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: How to Read Domain values using ABAP CDS Entity view
entry.content: @gforst Ideally that would be the better solution, but both the OP and I are on BTP ABAP Environment (as indicated by the OP's managed tags). In Clean Core, DD07L and DD07T are marked explicitly as not to be released for use. That means you cannot directly use these tables in other solution components like customer or partner solutions.

entry.title: Re: BAPI/FM to update PAN Number in BP tcode for Vendors
entry.content: do u know any bapi for mass update to pan number

entry.title: S/4 content federation to BTP
entry.content:
Hi All,

When i expose/update some S4 roles in the 2nd round, then the roles that were exposed in the 1st run are neither available in Content Provider nor in Site and nor in Role collection. Can you please advise if all roles will need to be exposed in every run.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Re: Electronic bank statement processing failed
entry.content:
Hi @erickcano ,

One of my colleagues just suggested that you should open an incident/ticket because that seems to be a system error, unfortunately.

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Disable checkbox selection list report page
entry.content:
Ok Tisha,

Part#3. CDS View with Expressions and Session Vari... - SAP Community

On the similar lines, could you please try

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZFIELDCTRL'
@EndUserText.label: 'Field Control for Report'
define view ZFieldControl as select from ZYourEntity
{
  key ZYourEntity.key,
  
  // Define expression for enabling/disabling a field
  // If field3 > 100, enable, otherwise disable
  case
    when ZYourEntity.field3 > 100 then 'ENABLED'  // Enables the field
    else 'DISABLED'  // Disables the field
  end as FieldControlStatus
}

Thanks

entry.title: Re: "Script Task" are now available in SAP Build Process Automation.
entry.content:
Hello
I have no access to property "custom" in the context

entry.title: Re: S/4 content federation to BTP
entry.content:
Yes,

All roles will need to be exposed in every run because when you add or modify roles in the 2nd round, those changes are synchronized to BTP, and only the newly exposed or modified roles will be updated in the Content Provider or Site

Thanks

entry.title: Clearing program J_3RCALD doesnt apply FIFO method
entry.content:
Hello,

We are having an issue following the FIFO method for customer payments, when a credit memo invoice is posted for a customer. The order of the documents to clear is changing , not following the FIFO method, when a credit memo exists. The credit memo is created with a reference to an invoice.

In the clearing program J_3RCALD, Partial payment is selected as clearing process, and in OB74 we have not selected any rule for account type D, leaving it blank.

How can the FIFO method still apply, even when a credit memo invoice is posted?

FBL5N:

In an example, the doc number in yellow, which is third in order to clear, according to the incoming payment, is not selected in the clearing program:

J3RCALD:

 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: How can we set default theme for a specific catalog o...
entry.content:
Hi,

Catalog-specific theme customization is not natively supported through the UI or Launchpad configuration directly.

I recall that we once applied a different theme to a specific tile, and it was done through a custom UI5 application.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Supplier Onboarding for SAP Sustainability Data Exchange (SDX)
entry.content:
@Elodie1 

Dear Elodie, 

The Pay-As-You-Go pricing model for BTP (the platform to use an app like e.g. SDX) is that you can use BTP for free as long as you stay a small solution like SDX. You find the details here: SAP Business Technology Platform Try and Buy

Yes, it is correct that the buyer who wants to collect PCF needs the base membership and the suppliers can download the free membership. 

The features for free memberships include:

- Productive tenants for SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.

- Limited functionalities for data provider only.

More details here: Pricing

Hope I could help. 

Warm regards, Jessica.

entry.content:
Hi All,

We are working on MDK application using OData service, while running the application on Your Dev Space (run as local web app) option in Business Application studio, we are facing some issues as mentioned in below screenshot:

This error not only belongs to that particular app, we are facing this one for others apps also.

Along with it when we try to run the app on Mobile Svcs app in Android we are not able to fetch data and getting this error:

 

 

Any help would be appreciated on this.

@bill_froelich 

Thanks and Regards,

Madhav

entry.title: Re: How to use HTTP Post method in SAP Gateway Client usi...
entry.content:
Hi,

Which is the key filed in this?

The error suggests that you're trying to update an existing record. Apart from the key field, can you please modify the values of other properties and try?

PATCH or PUT instead of CREATE you can try, also please ensure the correct Url.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: api_warehouse_order_task_2
entry.content: Hi @GeovanaLettnin - This is not correct answer, Please change the status. Kind Regards, Ajit

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

401 indicates Authentication issue.

Verify that the user has at least the "Viewer" or "Administrator" role in SAP Build Process Automation. Without the right role, users may not be able to access the process.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: S/4 content federation to BTP
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. if i understand correctly, If the existing S4 roles which are already uploaded in BTP are not exposed in 2nd run, they will be removed from Content Provider and Site. is my understanding correct. So, what is the max. no. of roles that can be uploaded in a run. And what if my system exceeds the max. allowed limit

entry.title: Re: How to use HTTP Post method in SAP Gateway Client using JSON format?
entry.content: You need to change the URL to pass the key (it's like GET). PATCH serviceRoot/entity(key). See how OData works.

entry.title: Re: Problem after migration TREX
entry.content: The problem was solved, by delete and recreate index on portal

entry.title: Missing annotations in metadata
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to create one app, that only show the data from different tables, this is to avoid having to create a separate (fiori element) app for each table. Or creating a new app for a new table. 
To achieve this we have several CDS view (for each table a CDS view) which will be expose in one oData service V2. For each table there is an interface (entity) view, a consumption (root enitty) view, metadata extension. Service definition looks like

@EndUserText.label: 'Service definition for different tables'
define service /UI_TABLES {
expose /C_Table1 as Table1;
expose /C_Table2 as Table2;
expose /C_Table3 as Table3;
......}

The xml view looks like

<mvc:View controllerName="test.controller.ListReport"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
     xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
  xmlns:smartTable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
  xmlns:smartFilterBar="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar">

    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <smartFilterBar:SmartFilterBar
            id="smartFilterBar"
            entitySet="Table1"  -> will be set/bind in the controller
            useToolbar="true"
            persistencyKey="SmartFilter"
            header="Filter">
        </smartFilterBar:SmartFilterBar>

        <smartTable:SmartTable
            id="smartTable"
            tableType="ResponsiveTable"
            enableExport ="true"
            useVariantManagement="true"
            useTablePersonalisation="true"
            entitySet="Table1"    -> will be set/bind in the controller
            smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
            header="Dynamic List Report"
            editable="false"
            enableAutoBinding="true" />
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

The problem we are facing is we are missing the annotations in the metadata file. The annotation @Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations is not set. Even when we set the annotations directly in the consumption view the annotations won't become visible in the metadata. 
The view looks like

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'test'
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
serviceQuality: #X,
sizeCategory: #S,
dataClass: #MIXED}

@search.searchable: true
@ui: {headerInfo: {
typeName: 'Option2',
typeNamePlural: 'test',
title: { type: #STANDARD, value: 'LastName' }
}}

define root view entity /C_OPTION2  as select from /I_OPTION2
{
@ui: {
lineItem: [ { position: 10, importance: #HIGH, label: 'ReportDate' } ]}
@search.defaultSearchElement: true
key SystemId,
@ui: {lineItem: [ { position: 20, importance: #HIGH, label: 'ReportDate' } ]}
@search.defaultSearchElement: true
key SysteemMandant,
@ui: {lineItem: [ { position: 30, importance: #HIGH, label: 'FullName' } ]}
@search.defaultSearchElement: true
FirstName,
@ui: {lineItem: [ { position: 50, importance: #HIGH, label: 'Days2exp' } ]}
@search.defaultSearchElement: true
LastName
}

We've tried:

  • annotations in consumption view / annotations in metadata extension
  • with and without @odata.publsh : true
  • with and without @vdm.viewType: #CONSUMPTION

Checking the oData service in segway client  xxxx /UI_TABLES_O2/$metadata no annotations are found for tableX. after changes we cleared the caches /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP and /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP.

What are we missing? 

Thanks in advance

Lee

entry.title: Re: Deploying the example cloud foundry project in the BT...
entry.content:
Hi @Jason_Taylor ,

 

Thank you for bringing this to our attention. This issue is already known, and possible solutions are outlined here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-foundry-runtime-learning-journey/issues/6. We will update the learning journey to prevent any further confusion.

Best regards,
Beyhan

entry.title: Re: Custom PPF action in S/4 HANA Public Cloud TM
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly refer to the Scope Item 1LQ for setting up Output Management. You can also refer to the Blog for Output Management in SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community to understand the concepts around this topic. You can also refer to the guided answers within the SAP for Me for help 2941522 - Output Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Guided Answer - SAP for Me

Regards,

Saurabh Goyal

entry.title: Re: Fetching Change request number with customer number o...
entry.content:
Hi All,
Thanks for helping. I used CDS View ( /1md/bpbp_headerobj ) for this and got the CR Number from BP Number and vice-versa.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content: From the Store, look for a project called "Process Actions" and install it.  Then try to add the "Process" again from the "Integration" tab. This seems to be a temporary work around.

entry.title: Re: How to get started in Career Corner?
entry.content:
Dear Careers Corner,

I hope this message finds you well.

My name is Alex, and I am reaching out to express my strong interest in SAP Logistics opportunities, particularly within the areas of SAP S/4HANA MM, SD, or related modules.

With over 20 years of experience in software implementation and training across diverse operational and business environments, I bring a solid foundation in delivering process improvements, stakeholder engagement, and systems integration. I have recently completed a Coursera SAP Technical Consultant course and am currently enhancing my expertise through a Wizcore SAP S/4HANA training programme.

I am now keen to transition into a dedicated SAP role—ideally in logistics or supply chain—where I can apply my practical experience and newly gained SAP knowledge to contribute value from day one.

I would greatly appreciate the opportunity to connect regarding any relevant contract or permanent positions, and I am open to roles across the UK or remote opportunities.

I look forward to hearing from you and discussing any suitable openings you may have.

Best regards,
Alex Ashley
www.linkedin.com/in/alex-ashley-6609938b 
alex.ashley4487@gmail.com
07984554780

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue

entry.title: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD Recurring journal entry mass posting (import function for recurring posting)
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for Finance, I would like to post recurring journal entries using a mass import functionality. Please advise if there is any available feature or planned enhancement that supports this requirement.

SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance FIN (Finance)

entry.title: BYD2502 Need to upload the PO Acknowledgement via API
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Need to upload the PO acknowledgement via API to the system. But there is no standard API available in the system. Can you please suggest how to process it via API. Please do the needful asap. Don't reduce the priority.



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2502 Need to upload the PO Acknowledgement via API
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

As discussed on the call, we will not be able to guide you to set up the entire communication arrangement and SOAP UI settings on how to execute. This is a pure consulting issue. 

However I am still happy to help you and share few information on PO Acknowledgement. 

You can use Web services for cross-system communication. Web services are Web-based application programming interfaces (APIs) that allow you to access and modify data in SAP Cloud solutions.

You can use Communication Scenario "Purchase Order Integration" for PO Acknowledgement. A communication system represents an external system which is used for application integration. Before you can use a communication system in an application integration scenario, you need to create a communication arrangement.  For more details regarding communication system, you can refer to help document  - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2dd3c6f8722d10148815c46f135628ad.html

Once the set up is done, you can go to API webpage - https://api.sap.com/api/PSM_ISI_R__SRMAP_II_LPURX_POP_PCO_IB/overview

You can select Manage Purchase Order Confirmation. This interface creates a purchase order acknowledgement based on acceptance or rejection of the requested products, quantities, and delivery period. 

Attributes:

Business object - Purchase Order Acknowledgment
Operations (Technical name) - Create purchase order confirmation (CreatePurchaseOrderConfirmation)
Predefined communication scenarios - Purchase Order Processing - Manage Purchase Order Confirmation
Process component (Application component) - Purchase Order Processing (AP-POP)
Technical name - PurchaseOrderProcessingOrderingIn

From API specification, you can download the WSDL file. 

If you go to API reference and Click on View Details, system will show the input parameters.

If you go to Documents tab and click on Business Documentation. This will open the help document for Inbound Service Manage Purchase Order Confirmation.

Here by you can find the sample Payload to Create Purchase Order Confirmation:

Create Request

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<n0:PurchaseOrderConfirmation xmlns:n0="http://sap.com/xi/SAPGlobal20/Global">
 <MessageHeader/>
 <PurchaseOrder reconciliationPeriodCounterValue="1">
  <ID>Token</ID>
  <SellerID>Token</SellerID>
  <Name languageCode="EN">Token</Name>
  <BuyerParty>
   <BuyerID>token</BuyerID>
   <SellerID>token</SellerID>
   <TypeCode>159</TypeCode>
  </BuyerParty>
  <SellerParty>
   <BuyerID>token</BuyerID>
   <SellerID>token</SellerID>
   <TypeCode>154</TypeCode>
  </SellerParty>
  <DeliveryTerms>
   <Incoterms>
    <ClassificationCode>Token</ClassificationCode>
    <TransferLocationName>String 1340</TransferLocationName>
   </Incoterms>
  </DeliveryTerms>
  <CashDiscountTerms>
   <Code>Token</Code>
   <FullPaymentDueDaysValue>999</FullPaymentDueDaysValue>
  </CashDiscountTerms>
  <Item>
   <ID>Token</ID>
   <SellerID>Token</SellerID>
   <AcceptanceStatusCode>AP</AcceptanceStatusCode>
   <Product>
    <BuyerID>Token</BuyerID>
    <SellerID>Token</SellerID>
   </Product>
   <ConfirmedPrice>
    <NetUnitPrice>
     <Amount currencyCode="EUR">50.0</Amount>
     <BaseQuantity unitCode="EA">1.0</BaseQuantity>
     <BaseQuantityTypeCode>EA</BaseQuantityTypeCode>
    </NetUnitPrice>
   </ConfirmedPrice>
   <ConfirmedScheduleLine>
    <SellerID>Token</SellerID>
    <DeliveryPeriod>
     <StartDateTime timeZoneCode="CET">2016-10-27T04:10:35Z</StartDateTime>
     <EndDateTime timeZoneCode="CET">2016-10-27T04:10:35Z</EndDateTime>
    </DeliveryPeriod>
    <Quantity unitCode="EA">10.0</Quantity>
   </ConfirmedScheduleLine>
  </Item>
 </PurchaseOrder>
</n0:PurchaseOrderConfirmation>

Refer to knowledge base documents pasted below; 

https://me.sap.com/notes/3463210

https://me.sap.com/notes/3354466

https://me.sap.com/notes/2765462

https://me.sap.com/notes/3574323

Hence closing this case with the above details. 

Best Regards,
Arun

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Library and SAP Build Apps Integration Issue
entry.content: Hi Shrinivasan, this works as a solution after adding "Process Actions" from the store, now I am able to enable process from Build Apps.

entry.title: sapbusinessonesdk.dll error after updgrade from sap b1 9.0 to sap b1 10. How can i resolve tihs ?
entry.content:
I've installed SAP B1 10.

I've an addon developed with VS 2019 (c#) and i used sapbusinessonesdk.dll as reference.

Now if i try to open the source code (with sap b1 10) i have an error and seems that sapbusinessonesdk.dll is not recognized.

What can i do ?

Thans

entry.title: Re: Tables 2LIS_04_P_ARBPL
entry.content:
Regarding "AFVG":
You can check CDS-View in newer S/4HANA systems: P_AFVG

This view consumes AFVC, AFVV, AFVU

AFVC
TableFrom
AFVV
TableInner Join
AFVU
TableLeft Outer Join

entry.title: Deprecation and Replacement of the Planning Filters App
entry.content:
Hi team,

Please help me to understand what are user-defined variants in SAP IBP which will get deprecated and need to recreate in new app as part of 2505 SAP IBP upgrade. Please find below link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/1a872737ea3647d28f59b3dc2e204292/411c196bba1f474eb4eec0da06c95993.html

 

where to find user-defined variants in SAP IBP which are used by business users ?

Thanks,

Srija

entry.title: Transactionsode: SOST automatically
entry.content: We recencly noticed in the SOST transaction that no message were being sent.  This was because the mail server had been disabled for message sending due a release update. After this was enabled, message sending worked. We are looking for an automatic way to receive a notification from the SOST if the sending fails. Does anyone know of a solution?

entry.title: How to Enable Client Certificate Authentication between CPI & SOLACE - End to End
entry.content:
How to Enable Client Certificate Authentication between CPI & SOLACE:

Steps:

  1. Create a Key Pair certificate on CPI Monitor -> Keystore(solace) with giving specific desired CN name. Please note that this CN name must be later used on SOLACE for additional steps.

2. Download the root certificate created in step#1 & provide it to the SOLACE admins.

3. In case of trial solace account, we would have admin access.

Navigate to Cluster Manager-> Create a service

Service -> Manage -> Certificate Authorities

Click on Add client certificate authority & copy paste the root certificate content on the dialogue box in solace & save it

4. Navigate to Broker Manager in solace & create a username under Access control with same name as CN name in step1

5. Enable the client certificate authentication in Access control under the settings tab

6. Create a queue & topic under the queue tab on broker manager

7. Create an IFLOW on CPI with receiver as AEM adapter. Provide the hostname, message VPN, username & authentication as client certificate authentication with alias name as “solace” created on step#1

 

8. Activate & deploy the IFLOW. The message will be pushed to Queue/topic created using the client certificate authentication

Regards,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 64 BIT Runtime only
entry.content:
Hi Jeff,

The error is due to you installing the EXE which integrates into Visual Studio which of course it looks for VS install location etc.

To redistribute the runtime go here:

https://origin.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/site/index.html

Select CR for VS and download the matching MSI packages

https://origin-az.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/file/0020000001375542024

And you may need the 32 bit MSI also:

https://origin-az.softwaredownloads.sap.com/public/file/0020000001375512024

PS - don't use the MSI packages on your DEV PC, the EXE will install them both.

Don

entry.title: Credit block re trigger during the ATP run
entry.content:
Hi,

Our client is facing an issue where an already released sales order will re trigger for credit block once the ATP is run. Usually once the production is done after couple of days from the sales order creation date, shipping specialist checks the availability of the item in the sales order using track sales order app. During the ATP run, sales order re triggers for credit block. 

This is in S4 Hana Public cloud 2502. Please let me know how we can avoid such behaviour? Unless in Private cloud, we cannot create a custom routine in the credit check rule of public cloud.

entry.title: Re: com.sap.it.rt.scc.connectivity.security.IToken.getPrincipalToken() - null value for 'tokenValue'
entry.content: I am facing the same issue. Did you get any solution?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Message Popover in Standard Pick by Cart F...
entry.content: Found a note, implemented the same. Issue is resolved, Closing the post. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports 2020 64 BIT Runtime only
entry.content: Thank you, Don. I was able to figure out that if I download the appropriate ZIP file in the "Crystal Reports, for Visual Studio", the runtime file I needed was there and I've installed it. This has allowed me to open the page that instantiates the CrystalDecisions objects, but yet to confirm the reports run. I had to disable 32 bit applications in the applicaiton pool to get it to work, which I do not in the original server. I accpeted it as part of CR 2020 being 64 bit only. Are you saying I SHOULD install the 32 bit, as well?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Message Popover in Standard Pick by Cart F...
entry.content: Implemented the SAP Note : 3279998 - Error messages are not displayed Pick By Cart. This resolved the issue.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule fqm_delete for snapshot cleanup in pu...
entry.content: For the automated deletion job if it is required to change the snapshot cleanup parameter "Number of Days Back to Present" a.) Go to the configuration activity "Define Basic Settings(SSCUI ID 101025)". b.) Drop down to the section "One Exposure Obsoleted Flows Processing Mechanism". c.) Select "Obsoleted Flows Processing Option" change to "C Customer Defined". d.) Update the "Days for Recorded Snapshot" to the required days.

entry.title: Re: Principal Propagation to SAP CPI using OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion
entry.content: Hi, I am sending the idocs to ECC via HTTP receiver adapter through cloud connector and I am using principal propagation. Could you please let me know in this case what all steps are required and do I need to generate the JWT token?

entry.title: DBA Cockpit Enhancements for Db2 12.1
entry.content:
The DBA Cockpit is a great tool for monitoring SAP systems on IBM Db2 for Linux, UNIX, and Windows (Db2 for LUW). It’s free and part of every ABAP-based SAP system.

The latest Db2 version 12.1, which was released by IBM in mid-November 2024, contains interesting features for SAP systems on Db2. Two weeks later, SAP certified Db2 12.1 for the use with SAP systems. So, as of November 29, 2024, you can install new SAP systems with Db2 12.1 or upgrade existing SAP systems to Db2 12.1 and explore the new features using the DBA Cockpit. 

Prerequisites

To fully exploit Db2 12.1, the DBA Cockpit needs a handful of ABAP correction instructions. SAP Note 3532234 provides an overview of required and recommend SAP Notes.

Please install at least the required SAP Notes which address adaptations to Db2 12.1 of existing functionality of the DBA Cockpit. The recommended SAP Notes enhance the DBA Cockpit and exploit the features of Db2 12.1.

Overview

The following screens of the DBA Cockpit were enhanced due to new features in Db2 12.1:

  • Configuration -> Overview
  • Configuration -> Encryption
  • Backup and Recovery -> Overview
  • Backup and Recovery -> Logging Parameter
  • Performance -> Transaction Log

Enhancements to Screen Configuration -> Overview 

The screenshot below shows the configuration overview screen after applying SAP Note 3138304. The Database section is completely new, as is half of the Database Instance section, too.

As of Db2 12.1, Db2 Advanced Log Space Management (ALSM) has become the default für SAP customers. The screenshot shows the two parameters ALSM Enabled and ALSM Status. ALSM Enabled tells you whether the functionality of ALSM is enabled in general. This is done via the Db2 registry variable DB2_ADVANCED_LOG_SPACE_MGMT=ON. However, the database configuration must fulfil certain criteria to allow ALSM to become active. E.g. log archiving must be turned on. Otherwise, ALSM doesn’t work. Subsequently, ALSM Status tells you whether ALSM is active and in operating mode for your SAP database.

With Db2 12.1, a focus was set to auditing on Db2 level in addition to the auditing capabilities of the SAP application server. You can now easily see if instance-level audit or database-level audit is activated.

Prior to SAP Note 3138304, the screen Configuration -> Overview displayed the HADR configuration, too. This was changed and a separate new screen was created which can be reached via Configuration -> HADR. As before, the information regarding HADR is only displayed if HADR is configured for your database. Otherwise, the screen is suppressed.

Enhancements to Screen Configuration -> Encryption

The next change comes with SAP Note 3484724. Prior to this SAP Note, the screen Configuration -> Encryption displayed details regarding database encryption. As of SAP Note 3484724, the screen was renamed to Configuration -> Security and six tabs were added to the screen. The tab “Database Encryption” contains the same information as formerly displayed on the screen Configuration -> Encryption. I briefly explain the five new tabs below.

Communication Encryption Tab

Here, the configuration parameters are displayed which are related to encrypted communication between the SAP application server and the Db2 database.

You can see that the relevant parameters for encrypted client server communication are not set and therefore no encryption takes place. Please also check the Connection tab (see below) that contains a table with the column Connection Security. This column shows the current encryption state for each connection to Db2.

Trusted Context Tab

As already mentioned above, auditing has moved more into focus. Auditing might generate a large volume of audit records. As Db2 12.1, you can establish trusted connections based on a trusted context with a trusted procedure. It is now possible to define an audit exception that allows you to suppress trusted connections from auditing.

Exclusively for SAP customers, Db2 delivers the trusted procedure SAPTOOLS.TRUST_PROC, which builds, in combination with the SAP application server, the foundation for this new capability. By default, SAP configures the trusted context. In the screenshot above, you can see the trusted context. In the table, there’s the column Exception Type. If the column shows “Audit Exception”, it means the exception is configured. Note that the audit exception is not configured by default by SAP but needs to be configured by SAP customers if they want to use this audit feature. For more information, see Db2 Audit and Audit Exceptions in the database administration guide for SAP on IBM Db2 for Linux, UNIX, and Windows on SAP Help Portal.

Connections Tab

This tab shows whether a connection uses client-server encryption and whether the connection is treated as a trusted connection defined via the trusted context, as explained above.

 “IMPLICIT” in column Trusted Context Type means that the connection is treated as trusted, which is the prerequisite for an audit exception. 

Db2 Audit Tab

On this tab, you get an overview of the audit configuration. You can see whether instance-level or database-level auditing is active, and it shows the granularity of database auditing in the table below.

 Find more information regarding Db2 audit in this blog post on SAP community: Using db2audit with SAP Applications

Users, Groups and Roles Tab

The Security screen is your central entry point to security-related configuration settings. The new tab Users, Groups and Roles provides you with authorization-related information: You can now see at a glance who has SECADM, DATAACCESS, or DBADM authority.

 This screen can also be helpful to validate if separation of duties is configured in your database. For more information, see Role-Based Security Concept for Database Users in the database administration guide for SAP on IBM Db2 for Linux, UNIX, and Windows on SAP Help Portal.

Enhancements to Screen Backup and Recovery -> Overview

With Db2 12.1, the following additional information is available in the history file:

  • Size of backup
  • Is backup encrypted?
  • Compression library used during backup
  • Does backup include log files?

SAP Note 3482193 provides the necessary adjustments for this. The table in the screenshot below shows the new columns Logs Included, Encryption and Compression Library.

Column Backup Size already existed in the table. However, prior to Db2 12.1, the backup size was retrieved and parsed from the file db2diag.log. The retrieval could be slow depending on the size of db2diag.log. As of Db2 12.1, the backup size is selected from the history file, which is much faster compared to db2diag.log.

 SAP Note 3521505 provides the same adjustments for archived log files that are displayed on the Archived Log Files tab on the same screen. As of Db2 12.1, you find the abbreviation “VE:” in the Comment column, which stands for “Validation Error”. This means that the checksum validation of the log file during arching failed. Archived log files with validation error are marked with a red background color in the table. 

If you double-click a backup entry in the table on the Database Backup tab, you can display details of the backup.

The Backup Sequences tab displays the sequences of a backup in the case that you performed a backup to multiple target devices in parallel. As of Db2 12.1, the screen displays the size of each sequence. The sum of all sequence sizes is the backup size. 

Enhancements to Screen Backup and Recovery -> Logging Parameter

In the previous section, I explained the term “Log File Validation Error”. Implementing SAP Note 3536120 enhances the section Log File Archiving Status with the Log File Validation field. The Log File Validation field shows the most recent log file number with a validation error. If no validation error happened in the past, “Success” is displayed.

 Enhancements to Screen Performance -> Transaction Log

Finally, SAP Note 3526895 adds the Log File Validation field also to the screen Performance -> Transaction Log of the DBA Cockpit. Note, however, that SAP Note 3526895 is only available via support package and not via correction instructions. If you haven’t yet moved up so far to the corresponding support package, you can display the Log File Validation field by implementing the two other options described above.

Summary

Db2 12.1 introduced a wide range of features around monitoring, configuration, auditing and trusted connections. I highly recommend reading SAP Note 3532234 and installing the required and recommended SAP Notes listed.

If you’re using the DBA Cockpit with Db2 11.5 or lower, you can also benefit from the enhancements to the Configuration -> Overview, Configuration -> HADR and Configuration -> Security screens. There’s no need to wait until your system is upgraded to Db2 12.1. You can apply the correction instructions now and benefit from the enhancements right away.

Finally, I’d like to thank my SAP colleagues Karen Kuck and Oliver Brein who contributed a lot to this blog post.

Let us know in the comments if you’re using the new enhancements in the DBA Cockpit and what you think of them.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Derive Value Restricted Measure Variable
entry.content:
Create an Input Parameter (IP)
Keep the Default Value Help as "Predefined Value from an Object"
Use the same IP in Expression editor with calculated measure

Create Variable in Analytic Model (AM) and Map it to the IP from backend fact view

Whenever user will access data via AM it will prompt automatically and as per input last week will be derived accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Order siblings in a Hierarchy with Directory with SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Sefan_Linders That's the approach we took but where we failed was getting that result to the table.

So we were able to generate a counter that tells us at what specific position in the daisy chain the node was. But we did not find a way to "emit" this to a result table. But possible that we went down the wrong route somewhere. This is the code that we used.

DECLARE current_id INTEGER := 1;
DECLARE next_id INTEGER;
DECLARE counter INTEGER := 1;



-- Looping through all NODEIDs (assuming they are all consecutive)
WHILE :current_id <= (SELECT DISTINCT COUNT(current_id) FROM Our_Remote_Table)
DO
    -- Resetting the counter after every NODEID to 1
    counter := 1;
    -- Count until NEXTID is NULL (or '00000000' in this case)
    WHILE next_id <> 0 AND counter <= 100
    DO
      SELECT TO_INTEGER(NEXTID) INTO next_id FROM Our_Remote_Table WHERE TO_INTEGER(NODEID) = :current_id;
      current_id := next_id;
      counter := counter + 1;
      -- No way to "emit" current_id or counter to a return table here or result set
    END WHILE;

END WHILE;

entry.title: Missing configuration items in Manage your solution
entry.content:
In the customizing app "Manage your solution" in the Test system, some configuration steps under the configuration item Electronic Communication - Statutory Reporting - Finance are missing. I can see them in the Productive system, however.

Configuration steps missing:

  1. Define Parameters for Electronic Communication with Authorities - 102367
  2. Make Settings for Electronic Communication with Authorities - 103726

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Crystal Reports Error: Invalid Printer Spe...
entry.content:
Hi Russell,

Need more details about what your app is doing at the time you get these errors?

What version of Windows are you using and does this happen on all PC's?

What Printer are you using?

Is your app a Server app or Desktop?

Do you know how many print jobs are currently being processed?

If you open the Printer task on the Task bar it should show you how many job's it's processing, maybe....

The first error is likely due to the communication layer from Windows to the Printer.

The second error is more likely the cause, the OS is having issues on the Printer Process thread.

Thinking out loud I suspect it could be the Printers print job Buffer is getting full, so when the next print job is sent it's being rejected, most errors coming from the OS are not very useful and generic...

Note the dump info there are no CR dll's being referenced, they are all Windows...

entry.title: The identifier is already defined in Datasphere SQL view
entry.content:
Hello Datasphere Experts

I'm having a problem that doesn't make any sense to me.

I created a SQL view where I perform a left outer join with a local table called NFPZ. Its semantic use is dimension, it's stored on disk, and it works correctly. This table is located in the same space as the SQL view.

The problem is that I'm getting an error like "The identifier "n_1" is already defined."

left outer join "NFPZ" as n_1 on
	nbew_op.mandt = n_1.mandt and
	nbew_op.einri = n_1.einri and
	nbew_op.falnr = n_1.falnr and
	nbew_op.lfdnr = n_1.lfdbw and
	(n_1.storn = '' or n_1.stdat = '00000000') and
	(n_1.farzt = '6' or n_1.farzt = 'E' or n_1.farzt = 'F') and
	not exists (
		select 1
		from "NFPZ" as n2
		where
			n2.mandt = n_1.mandt and
			n2.einri = n_1.einri and
			n2.falnr = n_1.falnr and
			n2.lfdbw = n_1.lfdbw and
			(n2.storn = '' or n2.stdat = '00000000') and
			(n2.farzt = '6' or n2.farzt = 'E' or n2.farzt = 'F') and
			to_int(n_1.lfdnr) > to_int(n2.lfdnr)
	)

Things I've checked:

I don't use that identifier anywhere else in the code.
The syntax of the entire code is correct and validated.

  1. I've tried removing the quotes, and it doesn't work.
  2. I've tried removing the alias that currently has "n_1," and it doesn't work.
  3. I've tried enclosing the alias in quotes (double and single), and it doesn't work.
  4. I've changed the case of the alias to lowercase, and it doesn't work.
  5. I've changed the order of the joins, and it doesn't work either.
  6. I've checked the table and its definition, and it's correct.

I don't understand this Datasphere error. I don't understand why no documentation appears, and I'm running out of ideas to fix it.

Could you please help me?

Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Re: Bringing Apps Onto SAP Fiori Launchpad - SAP S/4HANA User Experience
entry.content:
Hi @bharanidharan 

first of all: sorry for the late reply. Was the problem with displaying the video a temporary issue or does it still exist? Is it different if you try to open a direct link https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_1a3706f5 ? I have already used the external link but there may be some issues depending on client, browser, settings etc.

We plan to migrate the video to the learning.sap.com platform to ensure availability for everyone, but we have to shape the video before we can do that. I will let you know when the video will be available on learning.sap.com.

Best regards
Ruth

entry.title: Re: About Enabling Boosters,"Set up account for Workflow Management". in a BTP Trial Accou
entry.content: Hello Mustafa,

entry.title: Re: About Enabling Boosters,"Set up account for Workflow Management". in a BTP Trial Accou
entry.content: Hello Mustafa, the links do not work. I cannot see SAP Build Process Automation also which do I click now?

entry.title: Re: DMC Transaction Data to be Deleted
entry.content:
Hi,

till now there is no possibility to delete plant or data.

Try by opening a ticket to SAP.
